text:
Once upon a time in the sleepy town of Whispering Oaks, there lived an owl named Oliver. Oliver loved whispers. Whenever he caught one, he'd flutter closer. One autumn afternoon, Oliver heard the most whispered thing.

It came from Tiny twiglets atop an ancient oak tree. "I'd go anywhere."

Oliver flew closer. That night, he dreamed of adventures. He flew south, following voices. They led him through gardens where animals talked. Once he got lucky.

A fox with large ears smiled. "You’re wise."

With wisdom, Oliver heard about a jungle lost. He flew south some more. There, monkeys tossed acorns.

Oliver tossed one back. Birds liked it.

Days turned into nights. He sought sunrise. There, a wise old owl hid treasures.

"I’ll keep."

 treasures for everyone.

The owlets loved stories.

So Oliver told tales. They lived forever. And that's why whispers made woodlings glow.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
